mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in residential construction demand, triggering negative sentiment and downward price pressure on the S&P 500 within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in both supply capacity and consumer demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) over 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months during a late-cycle expansion confirms labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the unemployment rise fails to correlate with equity drawdowns in walk-forward validation (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects sustained consumer spending momentum, supporting near-term corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with positive retail sales growth, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index year-over-year growth exceeding 4.0% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and real return compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation months, or the threshold condition shows no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent labor signals where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicate deteriorating job quality and hidden labor market stress, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergent labor condition fails to predict negative returns (positive direction in >50% of cases), or interaction effects show no incremental predictive value over single-series baselines (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 2.0% annualized during a monetary tightening cycle signals economic momentum exhaustion, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of low-GDP quarters, or the signal loses predictive significance when conditioned on the tightening regime (Rank IC < 0.03).
